reasoning: Human Bocavirus is a viral infection that primarily causes respiratory tract illnesses in young children. My initial assessment is that creating and approving a new vaccine takes years and any upcoming approval within the next 4 months would be widely discussed in the news and medical literature, and would already be in late-stage Phase 3 clinical trials. Web searches revealed no evidence of a human bocavirus vaccine having been developed, let alone being anywhere close to approval or in clinical trials. It is not listed as a target in major mRNA vaccine pipelines. Furthermore, the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" indicates "No" under vaccines for Human Bocavirus, as of October 2025. Given the lack of any clinical trials for a bocavirus vaccine, the probability of one being created, approved, and updated on Wikipedia by the resolution dates (spanning Nov 2025 to Jan 2026) is near zero. The slight increase in probability for longer horizons merely accounts for infinitesimal technical/editing anomalies on Wikipedia.
